hokkaido.tagline: Hokkaido's hydrangeas bloom from July to August, with 7 stunning viewing spots across the region, a month later than Honshu.
hokkaido.desc:
Hokkaido's cool climate makes it the perfect backdrop for hydrangeas, which typically bloom from mid-July to mid-August, a month later than in Kanto and Kansai. This unique timing creates an exceptional summer flower-viewing experience.

There are 7 key spots across the region, ranging from Matsumae Park in the south to Nakajima Park and the Civic Forest in Sapporo. Each location showcases diverse landscapes and settings.

Highlights include Matsumae Park, famous for its variety of Somei Yoshino hydrangeas, which attracts numerous visitors from both within and outside Hokkaido each year. 

The best time to visit is from late July to early August when all locations reach their peak bloom, perfectly aligning with Hokkaido's summer travel plans. Given Hokkaido's vastness, it's advisable to focus on 1 or 2 areas for flower viewing to maximize your experience and minimize travel time.

aomori.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Aomori, blending nature and history along the Tsugaru Strait.
aomori.desc:
Aomori Prefecture, located at the northern tip of Honshu, enjoys a marine climate that allows hydrangeas to bloom from late June to late July. The relatively mild temperatures help extend the blooming period. There are five key flower-viewing locations scattered from Cape Tōhoku to various temples within the prefecture.

The most iconic spot is at Cape Tōhoku, where hydrangeas sway in the coastal breeze against the backdrop of the vast Sea of Japan. The ancient hydrangea trees at Mutsu Kokubun-ji are steeped in history, while Ishikawa Daibutsu Park showcases a magnificent stone Buddha surrounded by vibrant flower beds.

Due to the high terrain and strong sea winds around Cape Tōhoku, the blooming period is slightly later than in other areas of the prefecture, so it's recommended to visit in early to mid-July. Pair your flower-viewing with a journey along the Tsugaru Railway to fully appreciate Aomori's rich natural and cultural scenery.

iwate.tagline: Explore 6 stunning hydrangea spots in Iwate, with the renowned Mutsu Ajisai-en and Hachiyasan Tendai-ji leading the way until late July.
iwate.desc:
Iwate Prefecture is predominantly mountainous, with hydrangeas blooming from late June to late July. There are six key flower-viewing locations in the area, ranging from dedicated gardens to ancient temples nestled in the mountains, offering diverse landscapes and varying bloom times due to altitude differences.

Mutsu Ajisai-en is the largest hydrangea garden in the prefecture, boasting a rich variety of species. At Hachiyasan Tendai-ji, hydrangeas bloom along the ancient stone steps, creating a deeply spiritual atmosphere. Kawasaki Ajisai Park is a beloved local spot, meticulously maintained by the community, attracting visitors from both near and far each year.

The peak bloom period is around early to mid-July when most locations are in full bloom. Given Iwate's vastness, it's advisable to plan your route in advance, combining visits to Tendai-ji and Mutsu Ajisai-en on the same day to save on travel time.

miyagi.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Miyagi, from waterfalls to lakeside parks, blooming from late June to mid-July.
miyagi.desc:
Miyagi Prefecture boasts a mild and humid climate, with hydrangeas typically blooming from late June to mid-July. There are five key flower-viewing locations spread across the region, from the outskirts of Sendai to various towns, featuring both large national parks and serene mountain trails.

Akiu Otaki Botanical Garden, located in the suburbs of Sendai, offers a unique backdrop of cascading waterfalls amidst vibrant hydrangea clusters. The National Oirase Lakeside Park is expansive, showcasing a diverse range of hydrangea varieties. Meanwhile, Kago-bōzan Tajiri Ajisai Road winds through rolling hills, transforming into a breathtaking carpet of purple and blue each year.

For the best experience, visit in early July when most spots reach their peak bloom. Akiu Otaki Botanical Garden is easily accessible, making it perfect for a day trip from Sendai, while Kago-bōzan is ideal for hiking enthusiasts looking to combine flower viewing with scenic trails.

akita.tagline: Discover Akita's stunning hydrangeas at 3 must-visit spots this summer!
akita.desc:
Nestled on the western side of Tohoku, Akita Prefecture enjoys a climate influenced by the Sea of Japan, resulting in abundant summer rainfall that nurtures beautiful hydrangeas. The blooming season typically spans from late June to mid-July, and while Akita may not boast as many spots as other larger prefectures in Eastern Japan, each location offers its own unique charm.

Yunshoji Temple is the most iconic flower-viewing site in the region, renowned for its stunning clusters of blue hydrangeas that have graced numerous travel publications. The Orikata Hydrangea Path winds along the mountainside, providing a delightful natural experience. Meanwhile, Suiun Park serves as a lovely urban park where locals can enjoy the beauty of flowers on a daily basis.

The peak bloom occurs in early July, drawing large crowds to Yunshoji Temple. To avoid the rush, it's best to visit on weekdays during the busy season. Pair your flower-viewing adventure with a trip to the Oga Peninsula or Akita City for a well-rounded experience in the prefecture.

yamagata.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Yamagata, where culture meets nature from mid-June to early July.
yamagata.desc:
Yamagata's distinct four seasons create the perfect environment for hydrangeas, especially during the rainy season from June to July. The prefecture boasts five key flower-viewing locations, with a significant focus on temples and shrines, blending cultural and religious experiences into your floral journey.

Outai Monjudo is famously known as the "Hydrangea Temple," featuring a vast array of ancient hydrangea trees. At Sasano Kannon, hydrangeas line both sides of the approach, creating a picturesque scene. Meanwhile, Mogami Park (Hydrangea Garden) is one of the larger public flower-viewing areas in the prefecture, showcasing a diverse range of varieties.

The peak blooming period typically falls between late June and early July. Yamagata's transportation is primarily centered around the JR Ou Main Line, making Yamagata City or Yonezawa City ideal bases for day trips to enjoy the flowers. The blooming season in late June coincides beautifully with Yamagata's summer festival activities, making it a time worth noting.

fukushima.tagline: Explore 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Fukushima from mountains to coast this summer!
fukushima.desc:
Fukushima boasts diverse landscapes, ranging from inland mountains to the Pacific coast, with hydrangeas blooming from late June to late July. There are 5 key flower-viewing spots in the prefecture, featuring historic park sites, castle parks, and coastal shrines.

Shirakawa Sekijo no Mori Park showcases hydrangeas against the backdrop of ancient ruins, creating a unique historical atmosphere. Kamega Castle Park offers a beautiful contrast between its stone walls and vibrant hydrangeas. At Habori Yakushi, the flowers frame stunning views of the Pacific Ocean, making for a truly unique experience.

The peak bloom period is around mid-July. Given Fukushima's vast area, it's advisable to plan flower-viewing routes focusing on 1 or 2 concentrated regions based on your location. Pair your visit with sightseeing in Bandai Plateau or Aizuwakamatsu for a fulfilling itinerary.

ibaraki.tagline: Explore 6 stunning hydrangea spots in Ibaraki, blending spirituality and vibrant blooms from mid-June to mid-July.
ibaraki.desc:
Ibaraki Prefecture, located in the eastern part of the Kanto Plain, boasts a warm and humid climate ideal for hydrangea growth, thanks to the ample rainfall during the rainy season. There are 6 representative flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, including famous temples, expansive national parks, and historic shrines, offering a perfect blend of scenic beauty and cultural significance.

The Uraian Kannon (Yakuouji) is renowned as the "Hydrangea Kannon," featuring over 6,000 hydrangea plants within its grounds. The National Hitachi Seaside Park is vast, with the hydrangea area being just one part of its floral displays. At Daibou Hachiman Shrine, the hydrangeas create a rustic pathway adorned with blooms.

The peak blooming period typically falls between late June and early July. You can reach Uraian Kannon via the JR Mito Line, while the National Hitachi Seaside Park is conveniently located near JR Katsuta Station, making it easy to visit both in one day.

tochigi.tagline: Discover Tochigi's top 5 hydrangea spots, featuring breathtaking views from Ashikaga Flower Park and Taiheizan Shrine's approach.
tochigi.desc:
Tochigi Prefecture, located inland in Kanto, enjoys a warm climate that brings hydrangeas into full bloom during the rainy season.

There are five key flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, from the famous wisteria at Ashikaga Flower Park to the scenic approach of Taiheizan Shrine, each offering a unique experience thanks to their varying altitudes and landscapes.

At Ashikaga Flower Park, hydrangeas bloom alongside the renowned wisteria, creating a year-round floral spectacle. The approach to Taiheizan Shrine is lined with around a thousand hydrangeas, making it a significant flower-viewing destination in the region. Meanwhile, the hydrangeas at Washuzan Shrine bloom peacefully amidst the tranquil mountain forest.

The peak blooming season is around late June, so it's best to visit on a sunny day for the most stunning views. Ashikaga Flower Park is conveniently located near Ashikaga Station, making it ideal for travelers using the railway.

gunma.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Gunma, where vibrant blooms flourish from mid-June to mid-July.
gunma.desc:
Surrounded by mountains, Gunma Prefecture enjoys abundant rainfall during the rainy season, creating the perfect environment for hydrangeas to thrive. There are 5 key flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with the blooming period concentrated from mid-June to mid-July, showcasing unique features in mountain temples and parks.

Seiryuzan Kichijouji is renowned as the "Temple of Hydrangeas," boasting a rich variety of colors. Onishi Pond Hydrangea Park, located in the suburbs of Maebashi, attracts large crowds of locals each year. Meanwhile, Shimonita Hydrangea Garden offers a charming rural atmosphere in a mountainous area.

The peak blooming period typically falls between late June and early July. Gunma is also famous for its hot springs, such as Ikaho and Kusatsu, making it a great idea to combine your hydrangea viewing with a relaxing onsen trip for a more fulfilling travel experience.

saitama.tagline: Explore 6 stunning hydrangea spots in Saitama, perfect for a half-day or full-day trip from Tokyo!
saitama.desc:
Saitama, located right next to Tokyo, benefits from the rainy season, providing ample moisture for its beautiful blooms. With 6 representative flower-viewing locations spread across flatlands and hilly terrains, it has become a top choice for flower enthusiasts in the Tokyo area.

Kongendo Park is famous for its seasonal flowers, and during hydrangea season, it continues to showcase its year-round floral charm. The Ogose Hydrangea Path is renowned for its several kilometers of flower-lined roads, featuring tens of thousands of hydrangeas. Meanwhile, Mizu no Yama Park, situated in the hills, offers breathtaking views of the Chichibu Basin, where the floral scenery beautifully complements the mountainous landscape.

The peak blooming period typically occurs in late June. Ogose can be easily accessed via the JR Hachiko Line or the Tobu Ogose Line, making transportation convenient. Mizu no Yama Park requires a bus ride, so it's advisable to check the schedule in advance. Both locations can be arranged for a relaxed half-day or full-day flower-viewing trip from Tokyo.

chiba.tagline: Explore 8 stunning hydrangea spots in Chiba, with Honjo-ji and Mamenbara Plateau leading the way.
chiba.desc:
Chiba Prefecture features gentle terrain and ample sunlight during the rainy season, making mid-June to mid-July the prime time for hydrangea blooms. With 8 notable flower-viewing locations, Chiba ranks high among the Kanto region, offering a diverse range of landscapes from flat temples to hilly plateaus.

Honjo-ji in Matsudo City is famous for its over a thousand hydrangeas and is one of the oldest flower-viewing spots in the prefecture. Mamenbara Plateau, located deep within the Boso Hills, is renowned as the "Sky Hydrangea" with more than 40,000 plants. Mama Farm combines farm tourism with hydrangea viewing, making it perfect for family outings.

The peak bloom period is around late June to early July. Honjo-ji is easily accessible on foot from Matsudo Station, while Mamenbara Plateau requires a car, so it's best to leave early to avoid holiday crowds.

tokyo.tagline: Tokyo boasts 13 stunning hydrangea spots, making it a top destination for flower enthusiasts in Japan.
tokyo.desc:
Tokyo is home to one of the largest collections of hydrangea viewing spots in the country, featuring 13 iconic locations. From urban botanical gardens to expansive parks in the Tama region, these sites are widely distributed across the area. The blooming season typically runs from mid-June to early July, coinciding with the rainy season, which enhances the vibrancy and fullness of the flowers.

At Jindai Botanical Garden, you'll find a dedicated hydrangea display area with over 200 varieties. Kōbō-ji Temple is renowned for its approximately 8,500 hydrangeas that cascade down the hillside, making it a historic favorite for flower viewing. Showa Kinen Park, with its vast expanse, includes a hydrangea section that is a vital part of its diverse floral exhibits.

The peak blooming period is around late June, and most locations are easily accessible by train. You can take the Keio Line to Jindai Botanical Garden or the Tama Monorail to Kōbō-ji Temple. It's best to visit on a sunny day to fully appreciate the stunning colors of the flowers.

kanagawa.tagline: Kanagawa boasts 14 stunning hydrangea spots, with Kamakura Meigetsuin and Hase-dera leading the way, blooming from early June to mid-July.
kanagawa.desc:
Kanagawa's landscape stretches from coastal plains to the Hakone mountains, featuring a warm and rainy climate that allows hydrangeas to bloom from early June to mid-July. With 14 prominent flower-viewing locations, Kanagawa ranks among the top in Japan, drawing flower enthusiasts from all over the country each year.

Kamakura Meigetsuin, famously known as the "Hydrangea Temple," showcases a breathtaking path lined with blue hydrangeas, making it one of Japan's most iconic flower-viewing scenes. Hase-dera is home to around 2,500 hydrangea plants, offering stunning views of Sagami Bay from its elevated grounds. Along the Hakone Tozan Railway, over 10,000 hydrangeas bloom, creating a unique "Hydrangea Train" experience during the evening illumination.

The peak bloom period is typically from mid to late June, attracting large crowds to Kamakura's temples and shrines. It's recommended to visit on weekday mornings for a more serene experience. The Hakone Tozan Railway offers special services during the hydrangea season, so be sure to check the timetable in advance to plan your trip.

niigata.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Niigata, from coastal temples to national parks, blooming mid-June to mid-July.
niigata.desc:
Facing the Sea of Japan, Niigata Prefecture enjoys abundant rainfall during the rainy season, creating ideal conditions for hydrangeas to thrive. There are 5 representative flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with blooms peaking from mid-June to mid-July, offering a diverse range of landscapes from seaside shrines to mountain parks.

Kokuyou Echigo Hillside Park is the largest flower-viewing spot in the prefecture, featuring expansive hydrangea areas. Renge-ji Temple, perched on a high plateau on Sado Island, showcases a stunning interplay between hydrangeas and views of the Sea of Japan. Meanwhile, Tokamachi Ajisai Park is a local favorite in the southern hilly region, attracting visitors from across the prefecture each blooming season.

The peak bloom period typically occurs in early July. Kokuyou Echigo Hillside Park is accessible via shuttle bus from Nagaoka Station, while Sado Island requires a ferry ride, making an overnight stay advisable. Travelers who love the charm of remote islands should definitely include Renge-ji Temple on their Sado itinerary.

toyama.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Toyama, blooming vibrantly from mid-June to early July.
toyama.desc:
Toyama Prefecture is surrounded by mountains, and its Japan Sea climate brings abundant rainfall, creating perfect conditions for hydrangeas to thrive. There are five representative flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with the blooming period concentrated from mid-June to early July, ranging from multifunctional parks converted from ski resorts to traditional public gardens.

IOX-AROSA in Tonami City is a year-round tourist destination developed from a ski resort, famous for its hydrangeas in summer; Hananomori Tenjinyama Garden features a diverse range of hydrangea varieties; and the Kenmin Park Taikoyama Amusement Park, as a large prefectural park, attracts many locals to its hydrangea area every year.

The peak blooming period is around late June. Transportation in Toyama mainly relies on the JR Hokuriku Main Line and Toyama Chihō Railway, with buses or self-driving needed to reach each spot, so it's advisable to plan your travel routes in advance. You can also combine your flower-viewing trip with cultural sightseeing in Kanazawa or Takaoka.

ishikawa.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Ishikawa from Kanazawa to the serene mountains of Fudou-ji.
ishikawa.desc:
Ishikawa Prefecture is renowned for the traditional culture of Kanazawa, where the rainy season creates the perfect environment for hydrangeas to thrive. There are 5 key flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with blooms peaking from mid-June to early July, offering both urban and mountainous experiences.

In Kanazawa, Shiratori Street serves as a charming hydrangea walkway, lined with vibrant blooms. Kurikara Fudou-ji, nestled in the mountains, showcases hydrangeas beautifully juxtaposed with ancient temple architecture. While the Utsu-yama Iris Garden primarily features irises, hydrangeas also play a significant role in its floral displays.

The peak bloom period occurs in late June. Kanazawa is easily accessible, with Shiratori Street located in the city center, perfect for a leisurely stroll. For Kurikara Fudou-ji, driving or taking a bus is recommended. Be sure to combine your hydrangea viewing with visits to famous spots like Kenrokuen and Kanazawa Castle for a fulfilling travel experience.

fukui.tagline: Discover 4 stunning hydrangea spots in Fukui, featuring the serene Daian Zenji and scenic Asuwa Park, blooming mid-June to early July.
fukui.desc:
Fukui Prefecture, located in the Hokuriku region, enjoys ample rainfall during the rainy season, creating perfect conditions for hydrangeas to thrive.

There are 4 representative flower-viewing spots across the prefecture, with the blooming period concentrated from mid-June to early July. While the number of locations is relatively small, each offers a unique blend of cultural and natural beauty.

Daian Zenji is a renowned Zen temple in the Echizen area, famous for its hydrangeas, and hosts special open events during the blooming season. Asuwa Shrine, nestled at the foot of Asuwa Mountain, is known for its weeping cherry blossoms, but the hydrangea season also draws many visitors. Asuwa Park, perched high above, provides a stunning view of Fukui City, with hydrangeas blooming beautifully on the hills.

The peak blooming period is around late June. Daian Zenji is accessible via shuttle service from Fukui Station, making it a great addition to a cultural itinerary that includes Eiheiji (the head temple of the Soto school of Zen).

yamanashi.tagline: Explore 4 stunning hydrangea spots in Yamanashi, framed by the majestic Mount Fuji.
yamanashi.desc:
Yamanashi Prefecture is renowned for Mount Fuji, and its mild summer temperatures combined with ample rainfall during the rainy season create the perfect environment for hydrangeas to thrive. There are 4 key flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with the blooming period concentrated from late June to mid-July, featuring a mix of famous bridges, temples, and parks.

Saru Bridge Park is located near the famous arched bridge along the Kōshū Highway, where hydrangeas stretch along the valley slopes; Komuroyama Myōhōji, known as the "Temple of Hydrangeas," boasts a vast collection of ancient hydrangea trees; and Rakusan Park serves as an urban oasis within the Kofu Basin, providing a daily flower-viewing spot for locals.

The peak blooming period is around early July, making it an ideal time for outdoor activities. Yamanashi's transportation primarily relies on the JR Chūō Main Line, with easy access to each site via train and bus. Consider pairing your visit with the scenic Fuji Five Lakes or the Kōshū wine region for a rich Yamanashi itinerary.

nagano.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Nagano, blooming from mid-June to mid-July in a refreshing highland climate.
nagano.desc:
Nestled in an inland plateau, Nagano boasts cool summer temperatures, making it an ideal location for hydrangeas, which bloom from mid-June to mid-July. There are 5 representative flower-viewing spots across the prefecture, ranging from temples in the Ueda region to flower paths in the Matsumoto basin, with varying altitudes creating slightly staggered blooming periods.

Kōchō-ji features a rich variety of hydrangeas, while Shimyō-ji, known as the "Shinshu Hydrangea Temple," is the most iconic temple for flower viewing in Nagano. The Shioda Plain Hydrangea Path stretches along tranquil rural paths, exuding a peaceful countryside charm.

The peak blooming period typically occurs in early July. Given Nagano's vast expanse, it's advisable to choose nearby spots based on your starting point. Pair your visit with cultural sites like Ueda Castle Ruins or Matsumoto Castle for a travel experience that beautifully blends history and nature.

gifu.tagline: Discover Gifu's 5 stunning hydrangea spots, featuring the unique 20-kilometer Hydrangea Road in the Bandori area.
gifu.desc:
Gifu Prefecture boasts a diverse landscape, ranging from the Nōbi Plain to the Hida Mountains. With abundant rainfall during the rainy season, hydrangeas bloom from mid-June to early July. There are five key flower-viewing spots across the prefecture, primarily showcasing mountain temples and rural flower paths.

Sankō-ji (Hydrangea Mountain Temple) is famous for its vast cultivation of hydrangeas. The Hydrangea Road in the Bandori area is renowned for its approximately 20 kilometers of hydrangea-lined scenery, making it Gifu's most celebrated flower-viewing destination. The ancient hydrangea trees at Kaichō-ji add a historical touch, while the tranquil temple environment enhances the meditative experience of flower viewing.

The peak blooming period is around late June. The Bandori area is best accessed by car, and traffic can be busy during the blooming season, so visiting on weekdays is recommended. Sankō-ji is conveniently located near Gifu City, making it easier to reach.

shizuoka.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Shizuoka, with Shimoda Park boasting 3 million blooms in June.
shizuoka.desc:
Shizuoka Prefecture stretches from the Izu Peninsula to the Enshū Plain, featuring a warm and rainy climate that allows hydrangeas to bloom throughout June. There are five main flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with Shimoda Park at the southern tip of the Izu Peninsula being the most famous, showcasing one of the largest displays in Japan.

Shimoda Park is home to approximately 3 million hydrangeas, blanketing the hillsides. The park hosts the annual Shimoda Black Ship Festival during peak bloom, attracting numerous domestic and international visitors. At Fudasan Son'eiji, one of the three sacred mountains of Enshū, the hydrangeas beautifully contrast with ancient cedar forests. Hamamatsu Flower Park features a variety of hydrangea species, making it a key flower-viewing destination in the western part of the prefecture.

The peak bloom typically occurs in mid to late June. Shimoda is accessible via the Izu Kyuko Line and serves as a popular endpoint for travelers exploring the Izu Peninsula, making it a great addition to your sightseeing itinerary.

aichi.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Aichi, from coastal hot springs to ancient temples, peaking in June.
aichi.desc:
Aichi Prefecture, located in the heart of the Tokai region, enjoys a warm climate with ample rainfall during the rainy season, making it a perfect environment for hydrangeas to thrive. There are 5 representative flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, with the blooming period concentrated from early to late June, offering a diverse experience that includes hot spring gardens, ancient temples, and historical parks.

Higashihara Onsen's Ajisai no Sato is famous for its approximately 40,000 hydrangeas and hosts a flower-viewing festival every June, making it the largest hydrangea spot in the Mikawa region. Honkoji Temple, located in the Agata area, is one of the oldest hydrangea temples in the prefecture. Otsuka Seikaiji Historical Park is widely known as the “Hydrangea Temple,” preserving the ruins of an ancient temple while planting a large number of hydrangeas throughout the park.

The peak blooming period is around mid-June. You can start your journey to Higashihara Onsen from Toyohashi or Toyokawa, and combine it with a relaxing hot spring stay along Mikawa Bay for a delightful flower-viewing getaway.

mie.tagline: Mie Prefecture boasts 5 stunning hydrangea spots, with the longest blooming season in the Tokai region from late May to early July.
mie.desc:
Mie Prefecture, facing the Kumano Sea and Ise Bay, enjoys a warm and humid climate, allowing hydrangeas to bloom from late May to early July, making it one of the regions with the longest blooming period in Tokai.

Among the five representative flower-viewing locations across the northern Ise area to the southern Kumano area, Meika no Sato stands out as the largest hydrangea garden in Mie, featuring a diverse range of varieties. Hazu no Sato is known for its community-maintained flower paths, showcasing a picturesque rural hydrangea landscape. Miyagawa-Dowai Park, located along the banks of the Miyagawa River, offers a beautiful blend of hydrangeas and river scenery.

The peak blooming period is around mid-June. Mie's transportation mainly relies on the Kintetsu Nagoya Line, and visitors will need to use buses or drive to reach the various spots. It's recommended to combine your visit with a trip to Ise Jingu or a hike along the Kumano Kodo for a fulfilling travel experience in Mie.

shiga.tagline: Shiga Prefecture is renowned for the hydrangeas of Kongorin-ji, blooming beautifully from mid-June to early July.
shiga.desc:
Shiga Prefecture, nestled around Lake Biwa, enjoys a mild climate influenced by the lake, with hydrangea season peaking from mid-June to early July. There are two main flower-viewing spots in the prefecture, each rich in historical and cultural significance.

Kongorin-ji is a famous ancient temple known as a sacred site for Kannon in the western part of Lake Biwa. It is celebrated for both its hydrangeas and autumn leaves, attracting numerous visitors during the hydrangea season. Meanwhile, the Yogo Lake Hydrangea Garden, located on the northern shore of Lake Biwa, offers a serene lakeside view adorned with blooming hydrangeas.

The peak bloom occurs in late June. Kongorin-ji is near Aichigawa and can be reached by bus from JR Biwako Line's Aichigawa Station, while Yogo Lake is within walking distance from JR Hokuriku Main Line's Yogo Station. Consider pairing your visit with a trip to Hikone Castle or Nagahama for a fulfilling experience in the Lake Biwa region.

kyoto.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Kyoto, with Sanzen-in and Mimuroto-ji leading the way.
kyoto.desc:
Kyoto is home to numerous historic temples, where the rainy season's moist climate beautifully complements the blooming hydrangeas, creating one of Japan's most iconic cultural flower-viewing experiences. There are five main spots across the prefecture, with the peak blooming period from mid-June to early July, each temple renowned for its unique garden landscapes.

At Sanzen-in in Ohara, around 3,000 hydrangeas bloom on mossy ground, creating a serene scene alongside the Amida Triad statues. Mimuroto-ji is famous for its impressive collection of over 10,000 hydrangeas, featuring around 50 different varieties, making it one of the top hydrangea destinations in Kansai. Meanwhile, Yanggu-ji (Yayoi Kannon) is known for its hydrangea-adorned handwashing area, drawing many social media enthusiasts eager to capture the perfect shot.

The peak bloom typically occurs in late June. Sanzen-in can be reached by bus from downtown Kyoto, while Mimuroto-ji is a short walk from the Kintetsu Mimuroto Station. Expect crowds, so it's best to visit on weekday mornings.

osaka.tagline: Discover 6 stunning hydrangea spots in Osaka, with Kuan-ji and Katsuo-ji leading the way!
osaka.desc:
While Osaka is known for its urban landscape, the northern hilly region boasts rich natural environments, especially during the hydrangea blooming season from early June to mid-July. There are 6 key flower-viewing spots across the prefecture, easily accessible for half-day to full-day trips from downtown Osaka.

Kuan-ji in Ikeda City features over 5,000 hydrangeas, creating a serene temple atmosphere; Katsuo-ji, nestled in the mountains of Minoh, showcases hydrangeas alongside the unique "Katsu Daruma". For nature lovers, Osaka Prefectural Forest and Naka-dera offer a more natural flower-viewing experience, perfect for hikers.

The peak bloom typically occurs in late June. Both Kuan-ji and Katsuo-ji are conveniently reachable via Hankyu Railway, making it easy to combine your visit with a scenic walk to Minoh Waterfall.

hyogo.tagline: Explore 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Hyogo, from urban parks to serene mountain temples.
hyogo.desc:
Hyogo Prefecture spans both the Seto Inland Sea and the Sea of Japan, boasting a diverse climate. The hydrangea blooming season typically occurs from early June to early July. There are five notable flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, ranging from the former royal villa in Kobe to mountain temples in the Tanba region, showcasing strikingly different landscapes.

Kobe City’s Suma Rikyu Park, a former royal villa turned urban park, features neatly arranged hydrangeas that exude a royal garden atmosphere. The Aino Hydrangea Garden in Sanda City is renowned for its vast array of hydrangeas nestled in the mountains. Raikouji Temple in Tanba Sasayama, known as the “Tanba Hydrangea Temple,” attracts large crowds each year with its ancient hydrangea trees.

The peak blooming period is around late June. Suma Rikyu Park is accessible from JR Suma Kaigan Park Station, while Raikouji Temple is located in Tanba Sasayama, reachable via the JR Fukuchiyama Line. Consider pairing your visit with sightseeing in Kobe Harbor or the Tanba region for a fulfilling itinerary.
img: images/ajisai/神戶市立須磨離宮公園/1.jpg

nara.tagline: Discover Nara's top 4 hydrangea spots, with Yata-ji leading the way in a cultural floral experience!
nara.desc:
Nara Prefecture is predominantly mountainous, and the cool climate during the rainy season makes it perfect for hydrangeas, which bloom from early June to mid-July. There are four main flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, most of which are situated within historic temples, seamlessly blending the floral experience with Nara's rich cultural heritage.

Yata-ji (Yamato-Koriyama City) is famously known as the "Hydrangea Temple," boasting around 60 varieties and 10,000 hydrangea plants covering the mountainside, making it the most iconic hydrangea spot in Nara. Tsubosaka-dera (Namba Hōka-ji) is renowned for its combination of Indian-style Buddhist statues and hydrangeas. Ofusa Kannon, located in Kashihara City, features hydrangeas alongside roses, both highlights of the blooming season.

The peak blooming period is around late June. Yata-ji is within walking distance from Kintetsu Yamato-Koriyama Station, while Tsubosaka-dera can be reached by bus from Kintetsu Yoshino Line's Tsubosaka-yama Station. We recommend pairing your visit with cultural sightseeing at Nara's Great Buddha or Hōryū-ji to enhance your Yamato road trip.

wakayama.tagline: Explore 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Wakayama from UNESCO sites to hidden gardens, blooming from early June to late July.
wakayama.desc:
Wakayama Prefecture, located in the southern part of the Kii Peninsula, boasts a warm and humid climate that allows hydrangeas to bloom from early June to late July, making their blooming period relatively long in the Kinki region. There are 5 representative flower-viewing locations throughout the prefecture, ranging from UNESCO World Heritage sites to mountain gardens, showcasing a rich blend of culture and natural beauty.

Kumano Nachi Taisha is the heart of the UNESCO World Heritage site "Sacred Sites and Pilgrimage Routes in the Kii Mountain Range," where hydrangeas bloom along the ancient stone steps, steeped in history. Hanazono Ajisai-en, located along the Nakahechi route, is renowned for its vast fields of hydrangeas. Negoro-ji, an ancient temple of Shingon Buddhism, attracts many visitors each year during the hydrangea season.

The peak blooming period is around late June to early July. You can reach Kumano Nachi Taisha by taking a bus from JR Kisei Line Nachi Station; it’s recommended to combine your visit with a hike along the Kumano Kodo for a fulfilling trip in Nanki.

tottori.tagline: Discover Tottori's 3 must-visit hydrangea spots, featuring the expansive Tottori Hanakairou flower park!
tottori.desc:
Tottori Prefecture, located in the San'in region and facing the Sea of Japan, enjoys abundant rainfall during the rainy season, making early June to early July the prime time for hydrangeas. There are three main flower-viewing spots in the prefecture, and while they are few in number, Tottori Hanakairou stands out as a large facility showcasing the unique flower-viewing culture of the San'in region.

Tottori Hanakairou (Hōki Town) is one of Japan's largest flower parks, featuring an extensive display of hydrangeas with a rich variety of species. Aizukasa Hydrangea Park is a small garden meticulously maintained by the local community, exuding a quaint mountain village atmosphere. Meanwhile, Tottori City’s Mihagino Hydrangea Park offers an urban flower-viewing experience in the prefectural capital.

The peak blooming period is around mid to late June. Tottori Hanakairou is accessible via shuttle service from JR Bibi Line's Nezu Station, making it easy to combine visits with attractions like the Tottori Sand Dunes or Mount Daisen for a well-rounded San'in travel itinerary.

shimane.tagline: Discover 3 stunning hydrangea spots in Shimane, with breathtaking coastal views along the way.
shimane.desc:
Shimane Prefecture stretches along the Sea of Japan, benefiting from a rainy climate that provides ample moisture for hydrangeas during the plum rain season. There are three main flower-viewing spots in the prefecture, with the blooming period concentrated from early June to mid-July, featuring coastal flower paths and rural gardens.

The Miho Kain Hydrangea Path, located near the Miho Kain Lighthouse at the eastern tip of the Shimane Peninsula, boasts a vast array of hydrangeas planted along the coastal trail, making it a key flower-viewing destination on the San'in Coast scenic route. Harada Hanazaki-en is a privately operated flower garden, while Hatase Hydrangea Path offers a traditional flower path in a tranquil rural setting.

The peak blooming period is from late June to early July. Miho Kain can be accessed by bus from Matsue City, making it a great addition to a trip that includes visits to Izumo Taisha or Matsue Castle for a fulfilling San'in travel experience.

okayama.tagline: Discover Okayama's 3 stunning hydrangea spots, with Daizenji Temple leading the way, blooming from early to late June.
okayama.desc:
Okayama Prefecture, located in the sunny Sanyo region, enjoys a relatively dry rainy season, making it the perfect place to admire hydrangeas from early to late June. There are three main spots for flower viewing in the prefecture, primarily centered around temples, offering a rich religious atmosphere.

Daizenji Temple, famously known as the "Hydrangea Temple," features hydrangeas blooming along ancient stone steps and mountain paths, attracting numerous visitors during the flowering season. Chōhōji Temple showcases a beautiful contrast between its hydrangeas and ancient cedar forests. Lastly, Zaitsuji Temple is another renowned site for hydrangeas, hosting special openings each year during the blooming season.

The peak bloom typically occurs in mid to late June. Transportation in Okayama is mainly via the JR Sanyo Line, with buses or self-driving needed to reach the temples. We recommend pairing your visit with sightseeing at Korakuen Garden or the Kurashiki Bikan Historical Area for a fulfilling Sanyo travel itinerary.
img: images/ajisai/大瀧山西法院（紫陽花寺）/1.jpg

hiroshima.tagline: Discover 4 stunning hydrangea spots in Hiroshima from parks to mountain retreats, blooming from late May to early July.
hiroshima.desc:
Hiroshima Prefecture enjoys a warm climate, with the rainy season starting early, allowing hydrangeas to bloom from late May to early July. There are four main flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, offering a diverse range of experiences from urban parks to rural agricultural sites.

Hiroshima City Botanical Park is renowned for its systematic cultivation of various hydrangea species, providing both educational and scenic enjoyment. Flower village Hanamura is located in Shobara City and is famous for its large agricultural flower facilities. Sankien is a prefectural Japanese garden once used by the imperial family, attracting numerous visitors each blooming season.

The peak bloom typically occurs around mid-June. Hiroshima City Botanical Park is accessible via Hiroshima Electric Railway, while Hanamura requires driving or taking a shuttle bus, so it's advisable to check transportation options in advance. Pair your visit with world heritage sites like Itsukushima Shrine for a fulfilling Hiroshima itinerary.

yamaguchi.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Yamaguchi from Tokiwakoen to Ajisai-dera, blooming from late May to early July.
yamaguchi.desc:
Yamaguchi Prefecture, located at the westernmost tip of Honshu, enjoys a warm climate that allows hydrangeas to bloom from late May to early July, making it one of the earliest blooming regions in Japan.

There are five key flower-viewing locations throughout the prefecture, ranging from large urban parks to local temples, offering diverse scenic experiences.

Tokiwakoen (Ube City) is the largest urban park in Yamaguchi, attracting many locals and tourists to its hydrangea area each year. The Kakanoyaki Ajisai-en, managed by a renowned dessert brand, features an exquisitely designed garden that combines commercial tourism with natural beauty. Ajisai-dera (Hofu City), widely known as the "Hydrangea Temple," offers a rich religious atmosphere when its hydrangeas are in full bloom.

The peak bloom period is around mid-June. Tokiwakoen is accessible via shuttle service from Ube Shinkawa Station. Consider pairing your visit with Akiyoshidai and Akiyoshido or the historic streets of Hagi for a well-rounded Yamaguchi travel itinerary.

tokushima.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Tokushima from gardens to mountain trails, blooming from early June to early July.
tokushima.desc:
Tokushima's landscape is predominantly mountainous, and the rainy season brings abundant rainfall, creating a vibrant display of hydrangeas from early June to early July. There are five key flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, ranging from the Tokushima Prefectural Botanical Garden near the city to the picturesque flower trails in the rural mountains.

The Tokushima Botanical Garden features a dedicated hydrangea display area with a rich variety of blooms. The Uchitsuma Hydrangea Road, located in the southern mountains, is renowned for its stunning hydrangea scenery along the roadside. Meanwhile, the Kusayabu Hydrangea Village is a community-maintained garden that attracts numerous flower enthusiasts each year during the blooming season.

The peak bloom typically occurs in mid to late June. The Tokushima Botanical Garden is conveniently located in the suburbs of Tokushima City, while the Uchitsuma and Kusayabu areas require a car to access. Consider pairing your visit with a view of the Naruto Whirlpools or attractions related to Awa Odori for a fulfilling Tokushima travel itinerary.

kagawa.tagline: Discover Kagawa's 5 stunning hydrangea spots with breathtaking coastal views from Shiun-ji Temple.
kagawa.desc:
Kagawa faces the Seto Inland Sea, boasting a warm and sunny climate. Despite relatively low rainfall, the rainy season provides ample moisture for hydrangeas, which bloom primarily from early June to mid-July. The prefecture features five key flower-viewing locations, with harbor parks and mountaintop vistas as the main highlights.

At Shiun-ji Temple in Mitoyo, you can enjoy panoramic views of the islands in the Seto Inland Sea. The spring cherry blossoms and summer hydrangeas are significant floral events of the year. The Kamo-bekawa Hydrangea Dream Road is a picturesque riverside path lined with hydrangeas, creating a stunning scene each blooming season. Ban-no-Sho Park in Sakado City is an urban flower-viewing park within the prefecture.

The peak bloom period typically occurs in late June. To reach Shiun-ji Temple, it's recommended to drive or take a shuttle bus, so check parking availability ahead of time. You can also combine your visit with a trip to Kotohira-gu Shrine or an art tour of Naoshima for a fulfilling Kagawa experience.

ehime.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Ehime, from urban parks to rural gardens, blooming from early to late June.
ehime.desc:
Ehime Prefecture boasts a mountainous terrain and a warm Setouchi climate, making it the perfect setting for hydrangeas to flourish during the rainy season. The peak bloom period is concentrated from early to late June, with five representative flower-viewing locations scattered across the prefecture.

Matsuyama Comprehensive Park, located on the outskirts of Matsuyama City, features a well-designed hydrangea area that offers a panoramic view of the Matsuyama Plain. The hydrangea scenery around Michi no Eki Karari serves as a lovely rest stop for those on a driving tour. Shingu Ajisai no Sato, nestled in the mountains of Nanyo, is a rural garden meticulously crafted by the local community.

The peak bloom typically occurs in mid to late June. Matsuyama Comprehensive Park is accessible via public transport, while the Shingu area requires a car. Consider pairing your visit with attractions like Matsuyama Castle or Dogo Onsen for a fulfilling Iyo travel itinerary.

kochi.tagline: Explore 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Kochi from Makino Botanical Garden to Anan Waterwheel Village, blooming from mid-May to mid-June.
kochi.desc:
Kochi Prefecture faces the Pacific Ocean, enjoying a warm climate with an early start to the rainy season. Here, hydrangeas bloom from mid-May to mid-June, making it the first region in Shikoku to showcase these beautiful flowers. There are five notable flower-viewing spots across the prefecture, with Makino Botanical Garden being the most renowned.

The Kochi Prefectural Makino Botanical Garden, named after the 'Father of Botany' Makino Tomitaro, boasts a rich collection of hydrangea varieties, making it a must-visit for plant enthusiasts. Anan Waterwheel Village features hydrangeas planted along the canals, set against a backdrop of traditional waterwheels from the Edo period. Meanwhile, Niyati Hydrangea Street stretches for several kilometers along the national highway, creating a picturesque flower path.

The peak bloom period is around early June. The Makino Botanical Garden is accessible by bus from Tosa-Yamada Station, and visitors can easily combine their trip with sightseeing at Katsurahama or Kochi Castle for a fulfilling Tosa travel experience.

fukuoka.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Fukuoka, with the breathtaking blooms at Nanzan Kannon-ji stealing the show.
fukuoka.desc:
Fukuoka Prefecture is the largest urban area in Kyushu, with the rainy season starting earlier, allowing hydrangeas to bloom from late May to early July. There are five key flower-viewing spots across the prefecture, ranging from ancient temples in the mountains to urban parks, offering diverse options for flower enthusiasts.

Nanzan Kannon-ji, located in Sasaguri, is renowned as the 'Hydrangea Village of Kyushu,' attracting a large number of visitors each June as the hillside bursts into color. Takatori-yama Park in Kitakyushu serves as an important green space for flower viewing amidst the industrial city. At Hakozaki Shrine in Fukuoka City, the hydrangeas within the shrine grounds are also quite impressive, especially during the famous Hojoya Festival.

The peak bloom period is around mid-June. Nanzan Kannon-ji offers shuttle buses from Sasaguri Station, but it's wise to check the schedule in advance during peak season. Consider pairing your visit with a trip to Dazaifu Tenmangu or a scenic boat ride in Yanagawa for a fulfilling Fukuoka travel experience.

saga.tagline: Discover Saga's 4 stunning hydrangea spots, featuring Japan's largest hydrangea valley with 300,000 blooms from late May to late June.
saga.desc:
Saga Prefecture enjoys a warm climate, with the rainy season starting early, making the hydrangea blooming period peak from late May to late June. There are four main flower-viewing spots in the prefecture, with Mifuneyama Rakuen being the most renowned, attracting travelers from all over Kyushu.

Mifuneyama Rakuen (Karatsu City) is famous for its approximately 300,000 hydrangeas covering the mountainsides, where the sound of the waterfall harmonizes beautifully with the hydrangea scenery. It has been selected as one of Japan's top 100 spots. Takeo Onsen Kankou Mura combines hot spring facilities with hydrangea viewing, offering a relaxing tourist experience. Sugiyama Daishinji, known as the "Hydrangea Temple," is well-loved by locals, with its hydrangeas beautifully complementing the ancient temple.

The peak blooming period is around early to mid-June. You can reach Mifuneyama Rakuen by taking a bus from the JR Karatsu Line. Don’t forget to pair your visit with local delicacies like fresh squid from Yobuko or Saga beef for a fulfilling Saga travel itinerary.

nagasaki.tagline: Discover 4 stunning hydrangea spots in Nagasaki, where European charm meets vibrant blooms from late May to late June.
nagasaki.desc:
Nagasaki Prefecture enjoys a warm climate, influenced by the ocean, providing ample moisture during the rainy season. The hydrangea blooming period stretches from late May to late June. There are four representative flower-viewing locations throughout the prefecture, ranging from a Dutch-themed park to a Western-style garden from the Bakumatsu era, adding a unique cultural flair to the hydrangea experience.

Hausutenbō (Sasebo City) is famous for its European-themed park, hosting large-scale hydrangea events every year. Glover Garden (Nagasaki City) is a historical site where Westerners resided during the Bakumatsu to Meiji periods, showcasing hydrangeas blooming among the Western-style buildings, creating a unique blend of Japanese and Western aesthetics. The hydrangeas along Nakashima River near Megane Bridge are also a well-known flower-viewing spot in Nagasaki City.

The peak blooming period is around early to mid-June. Hausutenbō is accessible by a dedicated railway, while Glover Garden is located in the city center and can be reached by tram. Consider pairing your visit with a trip to Gunkanjima or enjoying the night views of Nagasaki for a fulfilling travel itinerary.

kumamoto.tagline: Discover 4 stunning hydrangea spots in Kumamoto from Suizenji Eitaku Park to Sumiyoshi Nature Park.
kumamoto.desc:
Kumamoto Prefecture enjoys a warm climate with abundant rainfall during the rainy season, making it the perfect backdrop for hydrangeas from late May to early July. There are four main flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, ranging from the famous Suizenji Eitaku Park in Kumamoto City to the natural parks of Amakusa, showcasing diverse landscapes.

Suizenji Eitaku Park is renowned for its rich spring water system, where hydrangeas thrive beautifully along the water's edge. Nishinokubo Park serves as an urban flower-viewing spot within the prefecture. Meanwhile, Sumiyoshi Nature Park in the Amakusa region offers a stunning view of hydrangeas against the backdrop of the shimmering Shiranui Sea.

The peak blooming period typically occurs around mid-June. Suizenji Eitaku Park is conveniently located near downtown Kumamoto, while reaching the Amakusa area requires taking the Amakusa Route bus or driving. Consider pairing your visit with a trip to Aso Volcano or the scenic Unzen in Amakusa for a fulfilling Kumamoto travel itinerary.

oita.tagline: Discover 5 stunning hydrangea spots in Oita, with the historic Usuki Stone Buddhas as the highlight during the blooming season in June.
oita.desc:
Oita Prefecture is famous for its Beppu and Yufuin hot springs, boasting a warm climate where hydrangeas bloom from early to mid-June. There are five representative flower-viewing locations across the prefecture, ranging from historical stone Buddha sites in the south to mountainous parks in the central region.

Usuki Stone Buddhas (Usuki City) feature rock-carved Buddhas dating back to the Nara period, classified as national treasures. Here, hydrangeas bloom in front of ancient statues, creating a unique historical floral landscape. Kyōzan Park serves as an urban flower-viewing park, while Tennō-ji Temple, located on the Kunisaki Peninsula, has a strong religious atmosphere, attracting both devotees and travelers during the hydrangea season.

The peak bloom occurs around mid-June. Usuki Stone Buddhas are accessible via a shuttle bus from JR Nippō Main Line Usuki Station. It’s recommended to combine your visit with the Beppu Hells Tour or a stroll in Yufuin for a fulfilling Oita hot spring travel experience.

miyazaki.tagline: Discover 3 stunning hydrangea spots in Miyazaki, with blooms peaking from late May to late June.
miyazaki.desc:
Miyazaki Prefecture faces the Pacific Ocean and enjoys a warm climate, with the rainy season starting early. The hydrangea blooming period is concentrated from late May to late June. Although there are only three main flower-viewing locations in the prefecture, each offers a unique charm that showcases the natural beauty and relaxed atmosphere of southern Kyushu.

Yamazakiguchi Ajisai Park is the largest hydrangea viewing spot in the prefecture, featuring a variety of species. Located on the Nichinan coast, Tōgenkyō Misaki boasts hydrangeas blooming on a promontory overlooking the Pacific Ocean, making it a rare seaside hydrangea spot in Japan. Heiwadai Park serves as an urban park in Miyazaki City, providing a daily flower-viewing location for locals.

The peak blooming period is around early to mid-June. You can easily reach the Nichinan coast via the JR Nichinan Line, and it's recommended to combine your visit with attractions like Aoshima Shrine or Udo Shrine for a fulfilling southern Kyushu travel itinerary.

kagoshima.tagline: Discover 6 stunning hydrangea spots in Kagoshima from Satsuma to Osumi, blooming from late May to mid-June.
kagoshima.desc:
Kagoshima Prefecture, located at the southern tip of Kyushu, enjoys a warm climate and has the earliest rainy season in Japan. The hydrangea bloom period peaks from late May to mid-June, making it one of the earliest prefectures in Kyushu for flower viewing.

There are 6 representative flower viewing spots across the Satsuma and Osumi Peninsulas. At Kaimon Sanroku Friendship Park, the stunning backdrop of Kaimon-dake (Satsuma Fuji) creates a breathtaking scene where hydrangeas and volcanic landscapes come together. Higashikaze no Sato (Hydrangea Garden) is a large specialized garden for hydrangeas in the prefecture. The hydrangeas around Osumi Lake reflect beautifully on the lake's surface, presenting a serene natural landscape of South Satsuma.

The peak bloom period typically occurs in early to mid-June. Kaimon Sanroku Friendship Park is easily accessible on foot from JR Ibusuki Makurazaki Line's Kaimon Station. We recommend pairing your visit with a sand bath at Ibusuki Onsen or a visit to Kirishima Shrine for a fulfilling travel experience in Kagoshima.

okinawa.tagline: Okinawa's Raohina Ajisai Garden is the only hydrangea viewing spot in the prefecture, blooming from mid-May to late June.
okinawa.desc:
Okinawa boasts a subtropical climate, with the rainy season starting earlier than anywhere else in Japan. The hydrangeas bloom from mid-May to late June, making this region the earliest in the country for these stunning flowers. The only notable viewing spot is Raohina Ajisai Garden, which, while modest in size, offers a unique hydrangea landscape amidst subtropical flora.

Located in the northern mountainous area of Okinawa Island, Raohina Ajisai Garden (in Nakijin Village) features thousands of hydrangeas blooming vibrantly among lush subtropical plants, creating a scene distinctly different from those found on Honshu. Visiting during the rainy season enhances the experience, allowing you to appreciate the beauty of the dew-kissed hydrangeas.

The peak bloom typically occurs from late May to early June. You can reach Nakijin Village by bus from Nago City; it's recommended to combine your visit with a trip to Nakijin Castle Ruins (a UNESCO World Heritage site) or Kouri Island for a fulfilling exploration of northern Okinawa.
